issues.title: User IDs exposed in URL query parameters
issues.description: buyerId and sellerId are passed as URL query parameters, exposing user identifiers in browser history and server logs.
issues.fix: Consider POST with JSON body for sensitive filter parameters, or document acceptable exposure level

issues.title: Missing input validation on amount filter parameters
issues.description: amountMin and amountMax are not validated (e.g. no check for negative numbers or non-numeric input) before being used in query construction.
issues.fix: Add number validation and min-value checks (e.g. must be >= 0) before applying as filters

issues.title: Date parameters not validated for ISO format
issues.description: dateFrom and dateTo are used directly without validating they are valid ISO date strings.
issues.fix: Validate date strings with a regex or Date.parse() check before applying to queries

issues.title: receipt_url rendered as href without URL scheme validation
issues.description: Database-sourced receipt_url is rendered directly as an anchor href. Low risk since it's admin-only and from trusted DB, but worth validating scheme.
issues.fix: Add scheme validation: only allow https:// URLs in receipt_url

issues.title: Excel export includes PII without additional protection
issues.description: Exported files include buyer/seller emails and transfer numbers with no watermarking or audit logging.
issues.fix: Consider adding audit log entry when export is triggered (who exported, when)

issues.title: No rate limiting on reports API with 10,000 row limit
issues.description: The reports endpoint can return up to MAX_LIMIT=10000 rows with no rate limiting, potentially enabling data enumeration.
issues.fix: Add rate limiting middleware or consider reducing MAX_LIMIT; add audit logging
